claims.title: Lambertian Model-Based Normal Guided Depth Completion for LiDAR-Camera System

claims.claim: Geometric angular features such as gradient and correlation provide a computationally efficient alternative to high-resolution spectral analysis for characterizing surface material properties at low angular resolutions.
claims.title: Lambertian Model-Based Normal Guided Depth Completion for LiDAR-Camera System

claims.claim: A random forest classifier leveraging these geometric features achieves 89.6% accuracy in distinguishing Lambertian from non-Lambertian surfaces, establishing a robust physical prior for guiding depth estimation networks.

claims.claim: Integrating this physics-driven material classification mechanism enables precise feature routing and mask generation that enhances depth completion performance in data-scarce outdoor LiDAR-camera systems.
claims.title: Lambertian Model-Based Normal Guided Depth Completion for LiDAR-Camera System
